title: Early years teacher degree apprenticeship: guidance for employers
body: "Information for employers on how to provide the EYTDA and support trainees to acquire the skills and knowledge they need to achieve early years teacher status (EYTS).  This guidance is to help:   early years employers understand the requirements and best practices for implementing the EYTDA in their settings training providers ensure the delivery of high-quality EYTDA courses  EYTDA mentors understand their role in being an effective mentor and support for trainees   It outlines key requirements and best practices for apprenticeship delivery, including:   funding information programme structure safeguarding mentoring and support end-point assessment entry criteria and recognition of prior learning graduate outcomes   Assessment form  Employers can use the form to assess the suitability of EYTDA trainees to work in early years level 3 staff:child ratios.  Candidate information  You can find out more about applying for an apprenticeship at Get Into Teaching.  "
